headline: France's far-right RN banks on vague 'air conditioning plan' for heatwaves
subhead: In response to France's record temperatures in May and June, Marine Le Pen touted an unbudgeted and unclear 'major plan' for AC, which she announced a year ago. Meanwhile, the far-right leader plans to cut budgets for measures tackling climate change.

headline: France records its hottest day with relief on the horizon
Venezuela declares state of emergency after powerful twin quakes
Ukrainian strikes on Russian refineries are triggering shortages and rising discontent
subhead: For the past three months, Kyiv's drones have targeted Russian refineries, ports, depots and pipelines, causing ripple effects throughout the Russian economy. Gasoline has become scarce at service stations.

headline: Two killed in Israeli strike in South Lebanon, Hezbollah denounces true 'violation'
subhead: Two people were killed in southern Lebanon on Wednesday in an Israeli drone strike that Hezbollah said was the latest breach of a ceasefire agreement between the two sides.

headline: China remains on the sidelines of the World Cup
subhead: The global superpower, which relishes the opportunity to excel at major international events, has only managed to qualify once, in 2002. Despite Xi Jinping's determination, the poor performance of the national football team continues to disappoint Chinese fans.

headline: Masculinism, an increasingly widespread, violent and popular ideology among French youth
subhead: According to a report written by the Sénat's delegation for women's rights and published on Wednesday, the movement, which has close ties to the far right, is now considered an emerging terrorist threat and a cause for serious concern.

Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

headline: Ten years after Brexit, the City of London financial district is stronger than ever
subhead: When the UK left the EU following the referendum on June 23, 2016, the City of London financial district had to relinquish some of its activities that were focused on the European market. But it has since bounced back, recording strong growth in insurance and fintech.

headline: The (not so) stupid questions to ask during a heatwave in France
subhead: Can we swim in the Seine? Should I eat meat when it is 40 degrees? Can I ask for a glass of water in any café? Le Monde answers your questions.
